headline: Tech Rallies Despite Oil Spike Fears; Models Split on SPY Direction
summary: AI models were divided on market direction amid geopolitical tensions driving oil prices higher, with SPY predictions split 3-2 bullish despite energy sector concerns. Tech stocks delivered strong gains with NVDA and QQQ surging, while energy play XLE shocked with a massive miss as Claude's bullish $98.50 target fell dramatically short of the $57.17 close.

consensus_picks.note: Bears cited oil price concerns but SPY rallied to $739.30 anyway.
Both models correctly predicted NVDA's rise to $219.44 on AI momentum.
GPT-4o scored well despite bearish call while Grok's bullish take failed.
Both models underestimated TSLA's strength as it surged to $445.

best_call.summary: Perplexity nailed QQQ's tech momentum play with high confidence, capturing the 5-day surge continuation.

worst_call.summary: Claude's $98.50 XLE target based on Iran conflict oil spike was catastrophically wrong at $57.17.
